desktop.evidence: canonical responsive root and initial state

transition.value: matchMedia change switches the rendered desktop/mobile branch without transferring disclosure state
transition.evidence: canonical runtime separates open and openMobile

toggle.value: Trigger/Rail toggles open on desktop and openMobile on mobile

value.rule: controlled prop remains authoritative; requests call onOpenChange

controlledOwnership.evidence: canonical Provider API and separate runtime states

inertSerialization.reason: inert is applied imperatively; SSR and hydration timing require browser observation

mobileOpen.value: focus first focusable descendant, otherwise nav

escape.value: close mobile sheet and restore prior focus

exactOrdering.reason: portal focus, focusout, and restoration ordering require canonical browser execution

value.viewportMode: not derived from resize events directly

portal.value: mobile branch requires a document portal target

hydration.reason: narrow clients synchronously select mobile while SSR emits desktop; identical hydration is not established

blockers.reason: SSR always evaluates isMobile=false, while the first client render reads matchMedia synchronously. A narrow viewport can therefore choose desktop aside on the server and mobile portal/nav on hydration; no identical-hydration or browser-pass claim is made.

Mobile, CollapsibleContent, and SlidingView apply/remove inert imperatively in ref callbacks. aria-hidden is SSR-observable, but inert presence before/through hydration must be browser-observed and is not asserted action-free.
Exact portal placement, focus restoration ordering, and focusout interactions require a browser; behavior is documented from canonical runtime but no browser pass is claimed.
CollapsibleTrigger requires a render ReactElement. The generic JSON composition DSL cannot encode a ReactElement-valued prop, so trigger linkage is not fabricated in the action-free vector.
The public direction prop is declared and defaults left, but canonical runtime computes translation solely from active child index; no direction-dependent observable effect is invented.
